_descriptor_type: A type of descriptor. One of 'simulation', 'trace', and 'perf'.
_architecture: A name of the available architectures (PARAMS.<architecture>) found in scarab repository, which defines the machine parameters of the default configuration.
_experiment: The name of the experiment. scarab-infra will create a new folder named containing all files needed to execute the simulation in the <ROOT_DIR>/simulations/<experiment> directory. The folder will also contain all generated simulation statistics/outputs.
_workload_manager: manual: run each exp scenario with all the simpoints at a time, slurm: Slurm schedules simulations based on its policy
_root_dir: Root directory mounted into the docker container home, for instance, the user's home directory
_application_dir: The path to the benchmark application directory used for tracing or simulation. This directory replaces ISO-based setups by mounting a local application folder (e.g., SPEC CPU2017) into the container during image build or runtime.
_scarab_path: The path to the scarab repository that contains the scarab binary used to run the experiment.
_traces_dir: The local directory containing all simpointed traces
_top_simpoint: Run only top 3 simpoints stored in 'workloads_top_simp.json' instead of the entire simpoints in 'workloads_db.json'
_simulations: A list of [suite, subsuite, workload, simpoint's cluster #, simulation type, warmup]. Available suite, subsuite, workloads, and each workload's available cluster_id and simulation_type are found in workloads/workloads_db.json. It runs all the simpoints if cluster_id is null and simulation type is one of 'exec', 'memtrace', 'pt'. When only 'suite' or 'suite'+'subsuite' is given and the rest are null, it launches simulations for all the workloads, all the simpoints if available, and in prioritized_mode (simulation type) in workloads/workloads_db.json. warmup can be null or smaller than the warmup of the trace available in workloads/workloads_db.json. The 'workload' field can also be a list of strings (e.g. ['gcc_r', 'mcf_r']) to run multiple specific workloads in a single entry, or an empty list [] to run all workloads (same as null).
_configurations: scarab configurations to sweep. slurm_options passes extra arguments to sbatch (ignored for manual runner); '--mem' is no longer needed here as memory is scheduled per-simpoint automatically. memory_overhead_mb (optional, default 0) adds a fixed MB overhead on top of the per-simpoint base memory for configs that allocate extra Scarab structures. Options: https://slurm.schedmd.com/sbatch.html

record._params: Timing-neutral record pass: attributes branch-stall cycles to feeder loads (writes <bench>_<simpt>_braddr.csv) and dumps the reused-missed non-feeder pool (<bench>_<simpt>_nfpool.csv) to the shared trace dir. IPC here equals baseline. Run this FIRST; the two oracle configs in disp_replay read these files.

_collect_stats: Stats collection settings for ./sci --collect-stats. configs: optional list of configuration names to collect (null or omitted = all configurations).
_visualize: Visualization settings for ./sci --visualize. Configure baseline and counters.
_mem: Memory scheduling and collection settings for Slurm jobs.
